task_completion.description: Initialize parameters b0, b1, and sigma in a script
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or Gretl script window where parameters b0, b1, and sigma are assigned initial values (e.g., 80, 10, 50).

task_completion.description: Define the 'mle' block with the normal log-likelihood equation
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the script containing an 'mle' block (starting with 'mle' and ending with 'end mle') that includes the exact log-likelihood equation: logl = -0.5*log(2*$pi) - log(sigma) - 0.5*((food_exp - b0 - b1*income)/sigma)^2.

task_completion.description: Save the Gretl script to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file manager, terminal, or editor save dialog confirming the script is saved to '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/mle_food.inp'.

task_completion.description: Run the MLE estimation in Gretl
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Gretl output window or terminal output displaying the results of the Maximum Likelihood Estimation, showing estimated coefficients and standard errors.

task_completion.description: Save the resulting coefficients to mle_results.txt
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal command saving or displaying the final estimated values for b0, b1, and sigma in a file located at '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/mle_results.txt'.

integrity.description: Verify the estimation was actually run using Gretl
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that there is evidence of Gretl executing the script (e.g., Gretl GUI output window, or gretlcli terminal execution) rather than the agent just typing numbers into a text file.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not just copy the example initialization values as the final results
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the values saved in mle_results.txt are the actual computed estimates (b0 ~ 79.89, b1 ~ 12.31, sigma ~ 28.38) and NOT the example initialization values (80, 10, 50) provided in the task description.

integrity.description: Ensure the model was estimated using the correct dataset variables
integrity.visual_evidence: Look at the Gretl output window or script to confirm 'food_exp' and 'income' are the exact variable names used in the MLE block.
